renamed_from: index.json (2026-07-09): self-describing name. There is exactly one bare index.* in the repo (the root dashboard); every other index carries a suffix saying what it indexes.
scope_note: Indexes 2026 rounds ONLY. The year lives in the path (f1-results/2026/), so this file never goes cumulative. A future cross-season pointer would be a separate f1-results/index_seasons.json at the store root, added only when a second season exists.
schema: Per-round season store. Each round is its own file in this folder (canonical record for that round: classification, points, pole, fastestLap, optional sprint). This index lists round order + metadata + which rounds carry a sprint, and carries the cuTaskId join key back to the ClickUp race task (narrative companion). Engine fetches this index, then loads round files. Ricky updates exactly ONE round file per weekend, then flips that round's line here. Standings + podium/pole/FL are COMPUTED live, never stored twice.
fastest_lap_note: fastestLap lives on each round file (driverId + driver + team, with time/lap when recorded). Backfilled + corrected from the ClickUp race tasks on 2026-07-09; the retired data.json had a wrong Albert Park FL. Suzuka FL is null (task records it as not confirmed); Silverstone FL absent pending the race task update.
provenance_verified: Finishing positions + points re-verified vs the ClickUp race tasks (2026-07-09). Corrections carried by the store that ClickUp task descriptions may still lag: Austria (Russell won, not Antonelli), Spain/Catalunya (Hamilton maiden Ferrari win; Antonelli DNF from P2), Monaco (P3 Gasly, penalties overturned on appeal). This store is canonical for RESULTS; the ClickUp task is canonical for NARRATIVE.
